title: Fix `disable iff` in simple properties
body:
Currently on master the disabled condition is included into the property expression. It causes two incorrect behaviors:
*  If the disabled condition is true, the assertion results in success. This behavior violates  the section 16.12. It should not result in success or failure and corresponding action blocks shouldn't be executed (16.14.1)
* Verilator adds sampling of its expression (because the property expression should be sampled), but it shouldn't do it by default (16.9.3 and 16.12)

This PR stops including the disabled condition to the property expression. Instead, when an assert is converted to an `if`, it wraps it into another `if` with the negated condition of `disable iff`. To do that, I had to defer the removal of `AstPropSpec` nodes to V3Assert.
